title: Business News: Tech, Startups, Silicon Valley
description: Get in-depth coverage of business news and trends at WIRED including technology, startups, and Silicon Valley.

items.title: The UK Places a Sweeping Ban on Social Media for Kids Under 16
items.content_text: The UK government is introducing a ban on social media for children and a minimum age for some chatbots in an attempt to shield young people from dangerous corners of the web.
items.content_html: The UK government is introducing a ban on social media for children and a minimum age for some chatbots in an attempt to shield young people from dangerous corners of the web.

items.title: Meta Tapped a Pentagon Supplier to Prototype Face Recognition for Its Glasses
items.content_text: Rank One, whose board includes a former CIA deputy director and a former FBI science chief, supplied face recognition to Meta for internal development of its smart glasses app.
items.content_html: Rank One, whose board includes a former CIA deputy director and a former FBI science chief, supplied face recognition to Meta for internal development of its smart glasses app.

items.title: A German Court Has Ruled That Google Is Liable for False Statements Generated by AI Overviews
items.content_text: The ruling holds that a company that designs, trains, operates, and manages an AI system must assume legal liability for any damages caused by the responses it generates.
items.content_html: The ruling holds that a company that designs, trains, operates, and manages an AI system must assume legal liability for any damages caused by the responses it generates.

items.title: Anthropic Says It’s Taking Claude Fable 5 Offline to Comply With US Government Order
items.content_text: “The government believes it has become aware of a method of bypassing, or ‘jailbreaking’ Fable 5,” the company said in a blog post.
items.content_html: “The government believes it has become aware of a method of bypassing, or ‘jailbreaking’ Fable 5,” the company said in a blog post.

items.title: Meta Employees Absolutely Hate Zuckerberg’s Plan for a Companywide AI Hackathon
items.content_text: “I’m not sure that this company supports a hackathon culture anymore,” one employee posted in a forum open to the entire staff.
items.content_html: “I’m not sure that this company supports a hackathon culture anymore,” one employee posted in a forum open to the entire staff.

items.title: ‘Tell Him He’s a Piece of Shit’: Meta’s New AI Unit Is a Total Mess
items.content_text: Executives and employees alike are struggling with Meta's chaotic AI strategy, according to sources and internal discussions reviewed by WIRED.
items.content_html: Executives and employees alike are struggling with Meta's chaotic AI strategy, according to sources and internal discussions reviewed by WIRED.

items.title: Chinese Drivers Are Using Tiny Plastic Heads to Fool Tesla’s Autopilot Safeguards
items.content_text: A cottage industry of celebrity figurines, blinking screens, and other DIY gadgets is helping drivers bypass Tesla's distracted-driving controls.
items.content_html: A cottage industry of celebrity figurines, blinking screens, and other DIY gadgets is helping drivers bypass Tesla's distracted-driving controls.

items.title: Elon Musk Is the World's First Trillionaire
items.content_text: SpaceX’s stock market debut has thrust the richest man in the universe into an unexplored frontier of wealth.
items.content_html: SpaceX’s stock market debut has thrust the richest man in the universe into an unexplored frontier of wealth.

items.title: Crypto Guys Bought the Answer to the CIA’s Mysterious Kryptos Sculpture
items.content_text: They swear they haven’t peeked at the closely guarded secret and that they’ll keep the cryptographic competition going.
items.content_html: They swear they haven’t peeked at the closely guarded secret and that they’ll keep the cryptographic competition going.

items.title: You Probably Won’t Get Rich Off the SpaceX IPO
items.content_text: The company has set aside an unusually high number of shares for retail investors. Still, experts say, you’re just getting the crumbs.
items.content_html: The company has set aside an unusually high number of shares for retail investors. Still, experts say, you’re just getting the crumbs.

items.title: SpaceX IPO Puts Elon Musk’s ‘Extreme’ Ownership to the Test
items.content_text: The rocket maker debuts on Nasdaq today under a wave of criticism about Musk’s near-absolute control. It’s how the company has worked from the start.
items.content_html: The rocket maker debuts on Nasdaq today under a wave of criticism about Musk’s near-absolute control. It’s how the company has worked from the start.

items.title: Why You Might Already Own SpaceX Shares, Siri’s AI Makeover, and Knicks Owner’s Surveillance Machine
items.content_text: Today on “Uncanny Valley,” we take an early look at the SpaceX IPO and why you might find yourself among the investors without even realizing it.
items.content_html: Today on “Uncanny Valley,” we take an early look at the SpaceX IPO and why you might find yourself among the investors without even realizing it.

items.title: ‘Hands Off Our NHS’: Anti-Palantir Protests Break Out in UK Over Deal With National Health Service
items.content_text: Crowding the gates of a major health care conference, protesters called for Palantir to be booted out of the UK’s National Health Service over privacy concerns and political grievances.
items.content_html: Crowding the gates of a major health care conference, protesters called for Palantir to be booted out of the UK’s National Health Service over privacy concerns and political grievances.

items.title: Meet the OpenAI Engineer Leading ChatGPT’s Biggest Transformation Yet
items.content_text: Thibault Sottiaux helped make AI coding one of OpenAI’s fastest-growing businesses. Now he’s overseeing a sweeping overhaul of ChatGPT.
items.content_html: Thibault Sottiaux helped make AI coding one of OpenAI’s fastest-growing businesses. Now he’s overseeing a sweeping overhaul of ChatGPT.

items.title: Grok Is Still Hosting Sexualized Deepfakes of Famous Women
items.content_text: A WIRED investigation found dozens of “nudified” deepfake images and videos on Grok's website, including nonconsensual depictions of celebrities and at least one prominent US politician.
items.content_html: A WIRED investigation found dozens of “nudified” deepfake images and videos on Grok's website, including nonconsensual depictions of celebrities and at least one prominent US politician.

items.title: Americans Are Trading Billions of Dollars on Polymarket’s Banned Offshore Platform
items.content_text: It’s the first estimate of how many Americans are sneaking onto Polymarket’s banned crypto-based platform.
items.content_html: It’s the first estimate of how many Americans are sneaking onto Polymarket’s banned crypto-based platform.

items.title: Welcome to the Waymo World Cup
items.content_text: It might not feel all that different from older World Cups—for better or worse.
items.content_html: It might not feel all that different from older World Cups—for better or worse.

items.title: People Living Near xAI’s Dirty Data Centers Are Furious About the SpaceX IPO
items.content_text: Elon Musk is set to make hundreds of billions even as communities in Mississippi and Tennessee are fighting to stop the gas turbines powering xAI's supercomputers.
items.content_html: Elon Musk is set to make hundreds of billions even as communities in Mississippi and Tennessee are fighting to stop the gas turbines powering xAI's supercomputers.

items.title: Anthropic Walks Back Policy That Could Have ‘Sabotaged’ AI Researchers Using Claude
items.content_text: The company changed course after researchers spoke out against the policy, which would have covertly limited Claude’s ability to develop competing AI models.
items.content_html: The company changed course after researchers spoke out against the policy, which would have covertly limited Claude’s ability to develop competing AI models.

items.title: A Meta Employee Who Just Lost Their Job Was Detained by Immigration Agents
items.content_text: Colleagues discussed the incident on internal message boards, according to documents seen by WIRED.
items.content_html: Colleagues discussed the incident on internal message boards, according to documents seen by WIRED.

items.title: Artificial Intelligence Sneaks Into the World Cup Thanks to Google Gemini
items.content_text: The Argentine national team will be Google’s test bench and technological showcase during the World Cup.
items.content_html: The Argentine national team will be Google’s test bench and technological showcase during the World Cup.

items.title: Anthropic Offers Mythos Upgrade for Cyber Partners and a ‘Safe’ Version for the Rest of You
items.content_text: Anthropic is releasing Claude Mythos 5 to trusted organizations and Claude Fable 5 to the public, a version it says can’t be used for cyberattacks.
items.content_html: Anthropic is releasing Claude Mythos 5 to trusted organizations and Claude Fable 5 to the public, a version it says can’t be used for cyberattacks.

items.title: The Untold Story of the Google Buses That Took Over San Francisco
items.content_text: A decade ago, commuter buses attracted big protests in San Francisco. Years later, the city is still feeling the repercussions.
items.content_html: A decade ago, commuter buses attracted big protests in San Francisco. Years later, the city is still feeling the repercussions.

items.title: OpenAI Confidentially Files for IPO on the Heels of SpaceX and Anthropic
items.content_text: The ChatGPT-maker announced it has filed paperwork to go public, just a week after rival Anthropic took the same step.
items.content_html: The ChatGPT-maker announced it has filed paperwork to go public, just a week after rival Anthropic took the same step.

items.title: Polymarket and Kalshi Say Influencer Partners Can’t Deny Election Results, Actually
items.content_text: Social media posts questioning the integrity of LA’s mayoral election were labeled “paid partnerships.” Then Kalshi and Polymarket told creators to delete them.
items.content_html: Social media posts questioning the integrity of LA’s mayoral election were labeled “paid partnerships.” Then Kalshi and Polymarket told creators to delete them.
